title: Static vs. Linked Memory Allocation
description: A visual comparison of how memory is allocated and managed in static arrays versus linked lists, highlighting the key differences in memory usage, insertion, deletion, and traversal.
relationships: Static allocation uses a fixed, contiguous block of memory assigned at compile time, while linked allocation uses dynamic, non-contiguous nodes connected by pointers
Inserting into a static array requires shifting elements, whereas inserting into a linked list only requires updating pointers
Deleting from a static array requires shifting elements to fill the gap, while deleting from a linked list only requires updating a single pointer
